SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
Envelope
The packet that travels between physical nodes through RabbitMQ.
Envelope.Type
The type of this envelope — determines how it will be processed.
Main
The main class for the physical node application.
Message
Represents an application-level message exchanged between physical nodes.
MessageHandler
A simple callback interface used to handle incoming messages Any class that wants to react to received messages implements this interface
NetworkConfig
Reads and stores the physical network topology from a JSON configuration file The configuration file describes: How many nodes exist in the network (nodeCount) Which nodes are directly connected to each other (adjacency matrix)
PhysicalHostService
The bridge between the physical layer and the virtual layer.
PhysicalNode
Represents a physical overlay node backed by RabbitMQ queues.
SpanningTreeManager
Manages distributed spanning-tree lifecycle for a physical overlay node.
SpanningTreeManager.Phase
Internal protocol phase.
VirtualEnvelope
The message format used exclusively between virtual nodes and their physical hosts.
VirtualEnvelope.Type
The type of this envelope — determines how it will be processed.
VirtualMain
Entry point for running a virtual node.
VirtualNode
A virtual node — a logical node that lives on top of the physical network.
